ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code of the city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the city */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the city */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the city above sea level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city for which the report is generated */,
  "max_temp" REAL /* Maximum temperature reported */,
  "min_temp" REAL /* Minimum temperature reported */,
  "current_description" TEXT /* Description of the current weather conditions */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the weather report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather forecast */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city for which the forecast is generated */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the weather forecast */,
  "expected_max_temp" REAL /* Expected maximum temperature */,
  "expected_min_temp" REAL /* Expected minimum temperature */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the expected weather conditions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hotels" (
  "hotel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hotel */,
  "hotel_name" TEXT /* Name of the hotel */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the hotel is located */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the hotel */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the hotel */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the hotel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hotel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hotels_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hotel_amenities" (
  "amenity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hotel amenity */,
  "hotel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hotel offering the amenity */,
  "amenity_name" TEXT /* Name of the amenity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the amenity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("amenity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hotel_amenities_hotel_id FOREIGN KEY ("hotel_id") REFERENCES hotels ("hotel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hotel_rooms" (
  "room_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hotel room */,
  "hotel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hotel offering the room */,
  "room_number" INTEGER /* Room number */,
  "room_type" TEXT /* Type of room (e.g. single, double, suite) */,
  "max_occupancy" INTEGER /* Maximum occupancy of the room */,
  "rate" REAL /* Room rate per night */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("room_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hotel_rooms_hotel_id FOREIGN KEY ("hotel_id") REFERENCES hotels ("hotel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bookings" (
  "booking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each booking */,
  "hotel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hotel being booked */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer making the booking */,
  "check_in_date" TEXT /* Check-in date for the booking */,
  "check_out_date" TEXT /* Check-out date for the booking */,
  "room_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the room being booked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("booking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_hotel_id FOREIGN KEY ("hotel_id") REFERENCES hotels ("hotel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_room_id FOREIGN KEY ("room_id") REFERENCES hotel_rooms ("room_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment */,
  "booking_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the booking being paid for */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g. credit card, cash) */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the payment */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date of the payment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payments_booking_id FOREIGN KEY ("booking_id") REFERENCES bookings ("booking_id")
)
